title: Exallage rigida
taxonomy: Exallage rigida is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is a type of shrub native to tropical Africa. Exallage rigida belongs to the genus Exallage, which consists of a few species of plants found in Africa. This classification helps identify the plant's relationships with other species in the same family. As a member of the Rubiaceae family, Exallage rigida shares characteristics with other plants in this group.

Its medicinal uses include treating digestive issues, fever, and skin conditions. intro: Exallage rigida, also known as Exallage, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Africa, belonging to the family Rubiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and digestive issues, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Exallage rigida include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Exallage is commonly prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is used in traditional medicine to treat fever, colds, and respiratory infections.
